steps.text: Let's visit the farm! Farms are home to lots of animals. Can you find them all?
Find the cow and tap it!
steps.feedback: Moo! Cows give us milk.
Horses are strong — farmers use them to help with work.
Quack! Ducks love splashing in water.
Rabbits love to hop and eat vegetables!
Dogs help farmers look after the other animals.

text: You know all the farm animals! Brilliant!

steps.text: Let's go on safari! These animals live far away in hot countries. Can you find them?

steps.feedback: Roar! The lion is called the king of the jungle.
Elephants are the biggest land animals on Earth!
Giraffes have the longest necks of any animal — to reach the tallest leaves.
Every zebra has its own pattern of stripes — just like a fingerprint.
Monkeys are very clever — they use their hands like we do!

text: Safari complete! You know so many animals!

steps.text: Everybody has feelings. Learning the words for feelings helps us talk about them. Let's find some feeling faces!
Who looks happy? Find the smiling face!
steps.feedback: A big smile! What makes you feel happy?
Sometimes we feel sad. That's okay — it's a normal feeling.
Worried means we're not sure if something will be okay. Everyone feels worried sometimes.
Angry is a big feeling. Taking a deep breath can help.
Surprised! Something unexpected happened.

text: You know five important feelings. Brilliant!

steps.text: Welcome to the fruit shop! Fruits grow on plants and trees — they're sweet and full of goodness. Can you name them all?

steps.feedback: Apples grow on trees. They can be red or green!
Bananas grow in hot countries on big plants.
Strawberries are red and grow close to the ground.
Oranges are juicy and full of vitamin C.
Pears are shaped like a teardrop.

text: The fruit shop is sorted! You know all those fruits!

steps.text: Vroom! These are all vehicles — things we ride or drive to get from place to place. Find them all!

steps.feedback: Buses carry lots of people at once — everyone sits together!
Trains travel on rails and can go very fast.
Tractors work on farms — they're very strong and slow.
Trucks carry heavy loads from place to place.
Diggers scoop up earth to make roads and buildings.

text: Vroom! You know all those vehicles!

steps.text: Some fruits come from far away in hot countries. These ones might be new to you — let's find out their names!

steps.feedback: Mangoes are orange inside and very sweet — they grow in hot countries like India.
Pineapples are spiky outside and sweet inside. They grow in tropical places.
Coconuts grow on palm trees. Inside is coconut milk — try shaking one!
Kiwis are green inside with tiny seeds. They're named after a bird from New Zealand!

text: You've been on a fruit adventure! Brilliant!
